tenders.title: Modeshift STARS
tenders.summary: Delivery of the Modeshift STARS programmes.  Refer UK2: Preliminary market engagement notice - Procurement Act 2023 Notice identifier: 2026/S 000-012356 Procurement identifier (OCID): ocds-h6vhtk-064d
tenders.detailedDescription: Delivery of the Modeshift STARS programmes.  Refer UK2: Preliminary market engagement notice - Procurement Act 2023 Notice identifier: 2026/S 000-012356 Procurement identifier (OCID): ocds-h6vhtk-064dc0  Sheffield City Council use the Modeshift STARS scheme to engage and work with schools on promoting, encouraging, and enabling active journeys to school.   The project aims to: • Engage more Sheffield schools in the project • Increase the number of accredited schools in Sheffield • Support schools to gain higher levels of accreditation • Increase the number of families choosing active journeys to school • Decrease the number of children being driven to school • Deliver active travel initiatives in participating schools across the city,  • Help schools to deliver their own active travel initiatives • Improve air quality around schools by reducing the number of vehicles used on the school run, and parking near the school gates • Make school entrances safer by working with other SCC projects and services (Parking Services, School Streets, Road Safety) • Coordinating and working with associated projects with mutual aims such as Living Streets, Move More, and Local Sports Partnerships.  We currently have 60 accredited schools, with 8 of those being at the highest level.

tenders.detailedDescription: This Research is to commission a one wave process evaluation of Community Accommodation Service Tier 1 (CAS1). tenders.title: Street Lighting Improvements Programme 2026 - 2030  - AWARD
tenders.summary: Sandwell Metropolitan Borough Council Highway Authority has a legal duty to maintain the highway network within the borough, including street lighting and illuminated signage. Sandwell MBC provides an
tenders.detailedDescription: Sandwell Metropolitan Borough Council Highway Authority has a legal duty to maintain the highway network within the borough, including street lighting and illuminated signage. Sandwell MBC provides and maintains 30,985 streetlights and approximately 5,000 other types of illuminated street furniture. Of these, 30,139 lighting units have already been upgraded through a previous lantern replacement programme. This delivered significant energy efficiencies, carbon reductions and introduced  LED lighting technology controlled via a Central Management System (CMS), a platform that enables remote monitoring and adjustment of street lighting assets for optimal performance and fault detection. The proposed Street Lighting Improvement Programme Contract, will focus on upgrading the small number of remaining non-LED lighting units and converting approximately 9,000 existing LED lanterns onto a single, integrated CMS. Additionally, the contract will enable the replacement of lighting columns and other illuminated street furniture that have reached the end of their design life, ensuring that the infrastructure remains safe and fit for purpose.

tenders.title: Downs to the Sea and Improving Pulborough and Amberley Projects at RSPB Pulborough Brooks
tenders.organization: ROYAL SOCIETY FOR THE PROTECTION OF BIRDS
tenders.summary: RSPB Pulborough Brooks is a large nature reserve found in West Sussex. It sits within the South Downs National Park and is part of the Arun Valley. Recent works on the reserve have meant we have good
tenders.detailedDescription: RSPB Pulborough Brooks is a large nature reserve found in West Sussex. It sits within the South Downs National Park and is part of the Arun Valley. Recent works on the reserve have meant we have good control of water levels, but there are still improvements to be made. New rills/foot drains will provide better feeding opportunities. Culverting round the willow bed will create a greater area to store water to help sustain the wetland into drier summer months. Bunding will ensure access around the site for management and grazing and will ensure hydrological compartments are clearly defined. This work will help make the site more resilient to the wetter winters and drier summers we are seeing as a result of climate change. Wet features and scrapes will help hold water during drier times and replacing broken culverts will create better water flow and control.

tenders.title: Downs to the Sea - Ferry Fields Predator Exclusion Fence

tenders.summary: Downs to the Sea is a partnership project led by the South Downs National Park and funded by the National Lottery Herritage Fund. Last year groundworks were carried out at Pagham Ferry Field. This yea
tenders.detailedDescription: Downs to the Sea is a partnership project led by the South Downs National Park and funded by the National Lottery Herritage Fund. Last year groundworks were carried out at Pagham Ferry Field. This year we will be installing a predator exclusion fence to protect ground nesting birds from Fox and Badger predation.

tenders.organization: ROYAL SOCIETY FOR THE PROTECTION OF BIRDS
tenders.summary: RSPB Pulborough Brooks is a large nature reserve found in West Sussex. It sits within the South Downs National Park and is part of the Arun Valley. Recent works on the reserve have meant we have good
tenders.detailedDescription: RSPB Pulborough Brooks is a large nature reserve found in West Sussex. It sits within the South Downs National Park and is part of the Arun Valley. Recent works on the reserve have meant we have good control of water levels, but there are still improvements to be made. New rills/foot drains will provide better feeding opportunities. Culverting round the willow bed will create a greater area to store water to help sustain the wetland into drier summer months. Bunding will ensure access around the site for management and grazing and will ensure hydrological compartments are clearly defined. This work will help make the site more resilient to the wetter winters and drier summers we are seeing as a result of climate change. Wet features and scrapes will help hold water during drier times and replacing broken culverts will create better water flow and control.

tenders.title: Corporate Business Travel
tenders.summary: The Authority is procuring a comprehensive Corporate Travel Management Service to support the booking and management of business travel across the organisation. The project aims to appoint an innovati
tenders.detailedDescription: The Authority is procuring a comprehensive Corporate Travel Management Service to support the booking and management of business travel across the organisation. The project aims to appoint an innovative supplier capable of delivering a high-quality, customer-focused service through both an online booking platform and an offline support function. The service will cover rail, accommodation, air travel, and car hire, ensuring travellers and customers have timely, appropriate, and cost-effective travel arrangements. The supplier will work collaboratively with the Authority to streamline processes, support policy compliance, control costs, and provide ongoing management information to monitor spend and trends. Key requirements include a fully functional online booking system, traveller profile management, integration with third-party providers, consolidated invoicing, and extensive management reporting. The system must also provide traveller tracking capabilities, emergency support, and be available 24/7 with agreed service levels for system outages and operational response times. The contract will run for three years from March 2026, with an option to extend by one year. Additional obligations include environmental support, data security compliance (GDPR, ISO 27001), training for the Buying Team, and support for future changes such as potential moves to self-service booking. The supplier will also be required to provide an exit plan and participate in regular contract management reviews to ensure continuous improvement and performance against KPIs.

tenders.title: Development of public consultation for UISPC
tenders.summary: The Unified Information Standard for Protected Characteristics (UISPC), commissioned by the Department of Health and Social Care (DHSC), is a comprehensive initiative designed to modernise and harmoni
tenders.detailedDescription: The Unified Information Standard for Protected Characteristics (UISPC), commissioned by the Department of Health and Social Care (DHSC), is a comprehensive initiative designed to modernise and harmonise equality monitoring across the NHS in England. The programme provides a detailed review of workforce, employment, and patient datasets, as well as associated national surveys, and provides robust recommendations for updating equality monitoring arrangements by reference to protected characteristics across the NHS in England. It is designed to comply with a complex range of intersecting legal obligations, including those provided by the Equality Act 2010 and intersecting provisions in the NHS Act 2006, data protection laws, other regulatory provisions and national standards. It also advises on how to bring current NHS data collections into legal compliance following the For Women Scotland Supreme Court judgment.

tenders.title: Analysis of NHS Cost Drivers and Disparities Refresh (Year 2)
tenders.summary: The NHS Cost Drivers and Cost Disparities project aims to help trusts analyse their National Cost Collection (NCC) data to identify variations and disparities in costing that impact productivity. Year
tenders.detailedDescription: The NHS Cost Drivers and Cost Disparities project aims to help trusts analyse their National Cost Collection (NCC) data to identify variations and disparities in costing that impact productivity. Year 1 of the project was designed to support the Secretary of States desire to be assured that the NHS understanding of its cost base and understanding of cost drivers. The NCC provides some, but not all of the information needed and to address this, a project was undertaken. This used the 2023/24 NCC data which was available at the time of Year 1 project. Year 1 was delivered as part of the Local Cost Collection (LCC)/ Federated Data Platform (FDP) work to enable NHSE to start building in these requirements to future improving the planned output from future cost submissions to support trusts to pinpoint inefficiencies and improve operational performance. It also successfully increased engagement with costing processes at both trust and Integrated Care Board (ICB) levels, supporting productivity improvements in line with the NHS Long Term Plan.
